instNote: ﻿This is a rapidly growing town, on account of the Florida railroad, which starts from there. A small wooden chapel has been erected under the patronage of St. Michael, in honor of a missionary of that name who suffered martyrdonn in the island nearly 300 years ago. A large brick church required for the influx of people will be erected as soon as means can be procured.
﻿The small church that has been built there was destroyed by a storm last year. A new one will be erected immediately.
﻿in honor of Louis Cancer, who suffered martyrdom on the coast, upward of three hundred years ago
﻿The United States are building here an extensive fort, and many Catholics during the winter are engaged on the works.

﻿This is the post where the few Indians left in Florida can be visited by the missionary. It is believed that they show a preference for the Catholic worship, as they have lately rejected the teachings of a Methodist preacher. Efforts will be made to visit them soon from Key West.
﻿A colony of Sisters of Mercy from the Diocese of Hartford came to St. Augustine last year,

﻿"among the young ladies of St. Augustine, for the promotion of religious music."
﻿"has been lately organized in St. Augustine, to promote religious instruction, morality and piety among the colored men."
